The enable_pdf_viewer block compiles //chrome/browser/pdf sources
(pdf_extension_util.cc, chrome_pdf_stream_delegate.cc, ...) which
include the grit-generated chrome/grit/pdf_resources.h and

Upstream's chrome/browser/pdf:pdf source_set lists this dep
explicitly; add it here to match.

author: Aaddrick
message:
fix: report display_mode to Blink for WCO, frameless, and fullscreen windows (#51396)

* fix: report display_mode to Blink for WCO and frameless windows

The WebContents delegate did not override GetDisplayMode(), so the
display_mode web preference fell through to Chromium's default of
kBrowser. matchMedia('(display-mode: window-controls-overlay)') and
matchMedia('(display-mode: standalone)') therefore always returned
false, even though the other three WCO signals
(navigator.windowControlsOverlay.visible, getTitlebarAreaRect(),
env(titlebar-area-*)) correctly reported WCO active.

Override GetDisplayMode() to consult IsFullscreen,
IsWindowControlsOverlayEnabled, and has_frame on the owner window,
mirroring Chromium's PWA cascade. The IsWindowControlsOverlayEnabled
gate is the same one that powers the other three signals, so all
four now agree by construction.

* fix: report display_mode to Blink for WCO, frameless, and fullscreen windows

The WebContents delegate did not override GetDisplayMode(), so the
display_mode web preference fell through to Chromium's default of
kBrowser. matchMedia('(display-mode: window-controls-overlay)') and
matchMedia('(display-mode: standalone)') therefore always returned
false, even though the other three WCO signals
(navigator.windowControlsOverlay.visible, getTitlebarAreaRect(),
env(titlebar-area-*)) correctly reported WCO active.

Override GetDisplayMode() to consult IsFullscreen,
IsWindowControlsOverlayEnabled, and has_frame on the owner window,
mirroring Chromium's PWA cascade. The IsWindowControlsOverlayEnabled
gate is the same one that powers the other three signals, so all
four now agree by construction.

For native fullscreen, display_mode reaches Blink via VisualProperties
rather than WebPreferences, so the (display-mode: fullscreen) media
query only re-evaluates when visual properties are synced. The natural
resize cascade picks this up on X11 but not on Wayland. Call
SynchronizeVisualProperties() from BrowserWindow's enter/leave
fullscreen observers so matchMedia stays in sync on all platforms.

Notes: Fixed matchMedia('(display-mode: ...)') reporting the wrong
value for window-controls-overlay, standalone (frameless), and
fullscreen BrowserWindow states.

author: Samuel Attard
message:
fix: read asar contents through the archive's retained file handle (#52283)

* fix: read asar contents through the archive's retained file handle

Reads of packed asar contents re-opened the archive by path on every
read, while using file offsets and integrity hashes from the cached
header. If the asar on disk was replaced while the app was running
(e.g. by an updater or MDM software swapping the app bundle), the
fresh open would read the new file's bytes at the old header's
offsets, producing either a fatal integrity-check crash in
ValidateIntegrityOrDie (browser process) or a spurious ENOENT preload
error when the file was briefly missing.

Read through the base::File the Archive has held open since the
header was validated instead:

- Archive::ReadFileAt() does positional reads on the retained handle;
  asar::ReadFileToString() now uses it, fixing the preload startup
  data paths and nativeImage reads.
- Archive::DuplicateFile() hands streaming consumers a duplicate of
  the retained handle; AsarURLLoader now uses it for packed files
  instead of re-opening by path, fixing the equivalent block-hash
  FATAL in AsarFileValidator for file:// loads.

This matches the JS fs wrapper, which has always read through the
archive's retained fd. Content served after a swap is the version the
app launched with, consistently, until relaunch. In-place tampering is
still caught: the retained handle sees modified bytes and the hash
check fails as before.

Also removes the now-unused Archive::path() accessor so future
readers of archive contents can't accidentally reintroduce by-path
opens.

* fix: lint

* fix: lint again

---------

Co-authored-by: Samuel Maddock <samuelmaddock@electronjs.org>

author: Samuel Attard
message:
build: only ship grit resources the binary actually references (macOS) (#52290)

Follow-up to #51804, enabling grit resource allowlisting on macOS. The
allowlist is extracted the same way as on Linux -- a --collect-inputs-only
pseudo-link scanned for ui::AllowlistedResource<id> markers -- except that
on macOS all native code links into the Electron Framework, so the
pseudo-link mirrors the framework's link inputs.

The chromium patch grows an equivalent --collect-inputs-only mode in
linker_driver.py (the Apple solink tool does not go through
gcc_solink_wrapper.py), and ar.py now keeps regular and Mach-O universal
archives in the scan list instead of silently dropping them -- on macOS
static libraries are regular archives, so their objects were otherwise
never scanned.

The allowlist check runs against the mac pak paths and gates
electron_dist_zip exactly as on Linux; no new suppressions or pak
additions were needed. Locale paks shrink from 47.1 MB to 8.6 MB
(arm64, uncompressed).

* fix: dangling additional data span in ProcessSingleton

ProcessSingleton stores the requestSingleInstanceLock() additional data
as a non-owning base::raw_span, but App passed a span into a local
blink::CloneableMessage that is destroyed when RequestSingleInstanceLock
returns. The span then dangles for the lifetime of process_singleton_,
which crashes under dangling-raw-ptr checks. Copy the bytes into an
App-owned member declared before (destroyed after) process_singleton_.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Skip-Lint: latent Electron dangling-span bug; no upstream Chromium CL to reference

* 7979322: Destroy ProcessSingleton window before releasing lock file on Windows.

The CL made lock_file_ a base::File and passes GetLastError() to base::File::OSErrorToFileError() on the failure path. Because the error was read after the enclosing scope's TRACE_EVENT0 and FilePath destructors ran, a failed lock-file CreateFile could surface as ERROR_SUCCESS and trip a DCHECK in file_win.cc. Capture the error inside the scope, immediately after CreateFile.

Ref: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/7979322
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update patches

* 7979322: Destroy ProcessSingleton window before releasing lock file on Windows.

CL 7979322 changed ProcessSingleton::lock_file_ from a raw HANDLE to a base::File, whose Close() runs a ScopedBlockingCall. Electron drives the singleton lifecycle from the UI thread (which disallows blocking) via app.requestSingleInstanceLock()/releaseSingleInstanceLock(), so destroying process_singleton_ tripped the blocking assertion on Windows. Allow blocking around the resets, mirroring the ScopedAllowBlocking the patch already applies to the POSIX destructor. Fixing it in Electron code keeps the change out of the Chromium patch.

Ref: https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/7979322
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude <svc-devxp-claude@slack-corp.com>

* chore: fixup yarn.lock, lint

* chore: fix build errors after rebase onto main

Remove stale `#include "shell/browser/native_window_features.h"` (header
was deleted in #51160) and add the required `animate` parameter to the
`SetBounds` call in `RestoreBounds` (signature changed on main).

* fix: rewrite virtual-display addon in Objective-C, removing Swift dependency

The CI runner's Xcode 16.4 has a broken Swift toolchain where
swift-frontend cannot load lib_CompilerSwiftIDEUtils.dylib. Since the
addon only uses CoreGraphics private C/ObjC APIs (CGVirtualDisplay,
CGVirtualDisplayDescriptor, etc.), rewrite the implementation entirely
in Objective-C to eliminate the Swift compilation step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <svc-devxp-claude@slack-corp.com>

* chore: remove nut-js dependent tests

* test: fix macos display tests

* Update docs/api/base-window.md

Co-authored-by: Sam Maddock <samuelmaddock@electronjs.org>

* Update docs/tutorial/window-state-persistence.md

Co-authored-by: Erick Zhao <erick@hotmail.ca>

* Update docs/tutorial/window-state-persistence.md

Co-authored-by: Erick Zhao <erick@hotmail.ca>

* fix: defer is_being_restored reset and persisted-state-restored to display-mode callback

Resetting is_being_restored_ synchronously at the end of RestoreWindowState
let the deferred display-mode callback (kiosk/fullscreen/maximize) fire from
Show() with the flag already false, so the resize/move events it produced
scheduled DebouncedSaveWindowState and persisted transitional bounds over
the user's saved bounds. Also rename the BaseWindow event to
persisted-state-restored consistently across the C++ emit, tutorial, and
spec, and move the 25s busy-loop spec into a spawned Electron fixture so
the spec runner main thread isn't blocked.

* test: bump main-thread-busy spec timeout to 60s

Spawned Electron fixture sleeps 25s plus startup, exceeding mocha's 30s
default.

* fix: emit persisted-state-restored synchronously, not in deferred callback

The previous fix moved NotifyWindowStateRestored() into the deferred
display-mode callback to keep it ordered after the kiosk/fullscreen/
maximize change. But that callback only fires from Show() via
FlushPendingDisplayMode(), so windows constructed with show: false (and
restore_display_mode_ true, which is the default) never emitted the
event - including the spec coverage. Restore the synchronous emit and
keep the is_being_restored_ deferral, since only the flag reset needs to
straddle the deferred resize/move events that schedule
DebouncedSaveWindowState.

* fix: tie window-state restore guard to display-mode transition lifecycle

The is_being_restored_ guard was reset on the deferred display-mode
callback invocation rather than the actual restoration lifecycle, which
caused three defects:

1. A window created with show:false and destroyed before show() left the
   guard stuck true, so the close-time FlushWindowState save no-op'd and
   state changes made while hidden were dropped.
2. showInactive() never flushed the deferred display mode, so a persisted
   hidden window shown inactive did not re-apply saved fullscreen/
   maximize/kiosk and left the guard stuck true.
3. On macOS the guard was reset synchronously after the async
   SetFullScreen/Maximize/SetKiosk calls, so a flush during the
   transition persisted transitional bounds over the saved state.

Reset the guard from the NotifyWindowEnterFullScreen/NotifyWindowMaximize
observers once the transition completes, drain a never-run callback in
FlushWindowState, flush the pending display mode from ShowInactive on both
backends, and skip saves while a fullscreen transition is in flight.

* test: update JWK error message assertion for unsupported algorithm

The error thrown for an unsupported OKP (Ed448) JWK changed from 'Invalid JWK data' to the more specific 'Invalid JWK OKP key'.

Ref: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/62499

* test: disable parallel/test-runner-v8-deserializer

This test synthesizes fake V8-serialized messages with the serialization format version hardcoded to 15 (0x0f). Electron bundles Chromium's V8, whose value-serializer format version is 16 (0x10), so the hardcoded headers are not recognized by the test runner's deserializer and the bytes fall through the UTF-8 stdout path (0xff -> U+FFFD), failing the assertions. The version difference is environmental and cannot be reconciled without re-patching the test on every V8 bump.

The failing subtests were introduced upstream in https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/62704 (test_runner: avoid hanging on incomplete v8 frames).

author: Keeley Hammond
message:
fix: enable GlobalShortcutsPortalPreferredTrigger by default on Linux (#52221)

Chromium gates the GlobalShortcuts portal off on GNOME behind this
feature, which makes globalShortcut a silent no-op on GNOME Wayland
unless apps discover and pass a Chromium feature flag. Enable it by
default; GNOME still consent-gates the first bind, and a user-passed
--disable-features=GlobalShortcutsPortalPreferredTrigger takes
precedence.
